scenarios.title: RefreshTriggerAdapter forwards request.MlvExecutionDefinitionId to the template renderer (string.Empty fallback when null)
scenarios.rationale: This is the wire entry for the multi-schedule feature: the adapter is the only place where the incoming request's MlvExecutionDefinitionId reaches the Scriban template layer. Forgetting this line silently disables the entire multi-schedule plumbing — the trigger_definition.json.scriban substitution would emit an empty string into the wire contract.

scenarios.title: RefreshTriggersTemplateRenderer constructor accepts mlvExecDefnId and assigns it to the MlvExecDefnId property
scenarios.rationale: The constructor parameter + field-assignment pair is the canonical wiring pattern for adding a Scriban template variable. Both lines must land together — adding only the parameter (without the assignment) would silently emit empty strings; adding only the property (without the parameter) would fail compilation.

scenarios.title: MlvExecDefnId property is exposed to Scriban via [JsonProperty("mlv_exec_defn_id")] — the wire-contract name templates reference
scenarios.rationale: The JsonProperty attribute IS the contract: Scriban templates reference variables by the snake_case JSON property name, not the C# PascalCase property name. A mismatch between the attribute string and the template's {{ ... }} placeholder would silently substitute empty — invisible until the trigger fires in production. The curator must surface this as a separate behavior from the property declaration itself.

scenarios.title: FMLVActivatorParser.FindFmlvExecutionDefinitionId walks steps[*].rows[FabricItemInvocation].arguments[parameters].values[*] to extract the fmlvExecutionDefinitionId parameter value
scenarios.rationale: The new parser method is the inverse of the template — it reads back the ID a previously-rendered ReflexDefinition serialized. Every guard in the LINQ chain (Where kind=FabricItemInvocation, FirstOrDefault name=parameters, paramName==fmlvExecutionDefinitionId) is a load-bearing contract with the Scriban template output shape. A curator must surface this as a single behavioural scenario; the 11 unit tests that probe each null-path are covered separately by s12.

scenarios.title: Parser only assigns action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId when Guid.TryParse succeeds — malformed values are silently skipped (action keeps default null)
scenarios.rationale: The TryParse + conditional-assign pattern is the defensive boundary: a malformed fmlvExecutionDefinitionId in the ReflexDefinition (e.g. a leftover from an older trigger format) must NOT throw a FormatException, must NOT poison the rule with an undefined Guid value, and must leave the rule's Action in a state equivalent to the pre-PR baseline (no exec defn id). The guard is the load-bearing back-compat invariant.

scenarios.title: trigger_definition.json.scriban substitutes {{ mlv_exec_defn_id }} (replacing the pre-PR {{ trigger_id }}) into the FabricItemParameter value field — the source-of-truth wire change
scenarios.rationale: This single-line template substitution IS the multi-schedule feature: pre-PR, every trigger received the trigger_id (always unique per trigger) as the exec defn id, which forced one-trigger-per-execution-definition. Post-PR, the field carries the actual mlvExecDefnId, decoupling trigger lifecycle from execution-definition lifecycle and enabling multiple triggers to fan out to a shared exec defn. Without this template change every other wire change in the PR is inert.

scenarios.title: fmlv_refresh_notebook.ipynb.scriban declares `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId = None` as a parameterized notebook variable
scenarios.rationale: This is the notebook-side input boundary: the Fabric notebook runtime injects the parameter by name. The `= None` default ensures backward compatibility with pre-PR triggers that don't carry an exec defn id, so the conditional s08 branch falls through to the legacy bodyless POST.

scenarios.title: Notebook builds a JSON POST body `{executionData: {mlvExecutionDefinitionId: exec_def_id}}` when exec_def_id is present; falls back to the legacy bodyless POST when absent
scenarios.rationale: The conditional branch is the load-bearing back-compat path: forgetting the else-branch would break every pre-PR trigger that doesn't carry an exec defn id, since the new path with `body` would still POST but with an empty exec defn id and the API would reject it. The branch is also the only place that injects `Content-Type: application/json` — without it the server would reject the JSON body.

scenarios.title: Notebook publicuri changed from query-string form `/jobs/instances?jobType=RefreshMaterializedLakeViews` to path-segment form `/jobs/RefreshMaterializedLakeViews/instances`
scenarios.rationale: The Fabric public API expects the jobType as a path segment when a body is supplied (the query-string form is the bodyless invocation contract). Mixing the two forms produces a 400 from the API. This change must land together with s08 — a curator must surface it as its own scenario because it is independently breaking: even pre-multi-schedule triggers will start hitting the new URL form.

scenarios.title: Notebook adds resp.raise_for_status() and guards Location-header presence before reading resp.headers['Location']
scenarios.rationale: Pre-PR the notebook would silently swallow 4xx/5xx responses (no raise_for_status) and would KeyError if the server returned a 2xx without a Location header — both bugs surface only under multi-schedule traffic patterns where the response shape can vary across job submissions. The defensive boundary is a separate behavior from the body construction (s08).

scenarios.title: Existing FMLVActivatorParser happy-path test extended with WithRule(fmlvExecutionDefinitionId: …) + Assert.AreEqual on rule.Action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId
scenarios.rationale: The pre-PR test asserted everything about a parsed rule EXCEPT its exec defn id (the field didn't exist). The new assertion locks in s05 — a silent revert of the TryParse-and-assign branch would now fail this specific assertion line, not just the new edge-case tests in s12.

scenarios.title: FindFmlvExecutionDefinitionId has comprehensive null-path coverage: empty steps, missing rows, wrong kind, no matching parameterName, missing parameterValue, missing values, etc.
scenarios.rationale: FindFmlvExecutionDefinitionId is a deep-LINQ JObject walker — every node along the chain can be null in malformed input. The 11 new test methods enumerate every null-path that the implementation guards. Without this coverage, a refactor that drops a `?.` operator would silently NullReferenceException at runtime under malformed input from an older ReflexDefinition format. The test suite is the regression net for s04.
